sound_type: kid contact call -- high thin mew 'meeeh'. 400-1,000 Hz. Separation distress. Alpine nursery. E=3
ecosystem: High alpine. Rocky cliffs, alpine meadows, snowfields. Summer. First year with nanny.
season: Summer (May-September). Born late May. First year development.

coordinates.description: Mountain Goat kid in Cascades alpine zones. Following nanny across cliff terrain.

taxonomy_note.description: Second Mountain Goat entry. Adult-kid pair (203-204). Second Bovidae.
taxonomy_note.family_count_in_engine: Bovidae: Mountain Goat Adult Bleat (203, E=4), Mountain Goat Kid Mew (204, E=3).
taxonomy_note.name_etymology: Oreamnos from Greek 'oros' (mountain) + 'amnos' (lamb). americanus for North America.
taxonomy_note.genus_note: Oreamnos -- sole living species. Kid = young of the year.
taxonomy_note.family_position: Bovidae: second entry. Adult-kid pair complete.
taxonomy_note.class_note: Class Mammalia. Alpine specialist. Precocial kid development.

song_metrics.fundamental_frequency_range_hz: 400-1,000 Hz. Higher than adult by roughly one octave.
song_metrics.song_frequency_hz: Kid mew: 400-1,000 Hz. Kid contentment: 200-500 Hz.
song_metrics.song_duration_s: Kid mew: 0.3-1.0 sec. Repeated every 3-8 seconds when separated.
song_metrics.note_structure: Single thin mew per vocalization. Repeated as contact/distress call.
song_metrics.note_types: Distress mew (high, thin, separation), contentment hum (lower, nursing).
song_metrics.phrase_structure: Repeated mews at regular intervals. Rate increases with distress level.
song_metrics.timbre: High, thin, wavering. Small body cavity. Small vocal cords. Not yet the resonant adult voice.
song_metrics.amplitude: E=3. Thin, high, infant-voiced. Quietest sound in the alpine. 50-200 m on stone.
song_metrics.tempo: Mew every 3-8 seconds. Faster when more distressed.
song_metrics.context: Separation from nanny. Visual contact lost on cliff terrain. First summer.
song_metrics.regional_variation: Minimal. Kid mews consistent across populations.
song_metrics.key_property: KID MEW. High thin. 400-1,000 Hz. E=3. ADULT-KID PAIR (203-204). Second Bovidae. Alpine nursery. Golden Eagle risk. v1.49.345.

pelage.overall: Kid: white wool, smaller version of adult. 3-4 kg at birth, growing to 15-25 kg by fall. Small horns emerging.
pelage.diagnostic_mark: Small white animal following larger adult (nanny) on alpine cliffs. Proportionally leggy. Small emerging horns.
pelage.seasonal_variation: Born with thin wool. Coat thickens through summer. By fall, well-insulated for first winter.

physical_description.overall: Kid at birth: 3-4 kg (7-9 lbs). By fall: 15-25 kg (35-55 lbs). White wool. Small emerging horns. Rubbery-pad hooves.
physical_description.diagnostic_mark: Small white animal on alpine cliffs. Always with or near nanny. Proportionally leggy. Climbing behavior within days of birth.
physical_description.field_id: Small white animal following larger white adult on alpine cliffs above treeline. If you see a kid, the nanny is nearby. Observe from distance.

nodes.title: Adult-Kid Pair -- 203-204
nodes.description: Same species across consecutive degrees. Adult bleat (E=4, nasal) and kid mew (E=3, thin). The -1 infant step consistent across families.

nodes.title: Alpine Nursery -- Precocial Development
nodes.description: Standing within hours. Climbing within days. Born on inaccessible ledge. Rubbery-pad hooves innate. The cliff is the nursery.

nodes.title: Eagle Paradox -- Survival Trade-off
nodes.description: Mew maintains maternal contact (survival) but attracts Golden Eagles (risk). Trade-off drives nanny vigilance and rapid response.

nodes.title: Cross-Family Infant Pattern -- Always -1
nodes.description: Fawn E=3/Buck E=4. Cub E=4/Adult E=5-6. Kid E=3/Adult E=4. The infant is always one energy step below the adult. Consistent.

cross_references.relationship: ADULT-KID PAIR! Adult bleat (E=4, nasal) to kid mew (E=3, thin). Same cliff. One octave apart.
INFANT PARALLEL! Fawn (E=3, forest) and kid (E=3, alpine). Both infant contact calls. Same energy, different habitats.
INFANT TRIO! Fawn (E=3, 195), cub (E=4, 202), kid (E=3, 204). Three infant voices, three families.
NURSERY AND NETWORK! Alpine nursery (kid) and music nursery (supergroup). New life in harsh terrain.
